units.title: My Body - Part 2
units.subtitle: Arms, Legs, Hands, Feet and Actions

lessons.title: Move Your Body
lessons.topic: Main limbs and the grammar of commands

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What part do you shake in the song?
questions.hint: You dance with these parts swinging side to side.

questions.question_text: Which part do you use to wave hello?
questions.hint: You lift it up and down to say hi!

questions.question_text: What part helps you see?
questions.hint: You open them wide to look around.

items.definition: At the end of your arm

steps.title: Ella's Fun Day
steps.text: Hi! I am Ella. I have two strong legs. I can stomp my feet. Clap your hands with me. Let's play! This is a happy day for our class.

questions.question: How many legs does Ella have?

steps.explanation: Use 'Clap your' to ask for clapping.

steps.audio_text: Hi kids! Are you ready to move? Clap your hands three times. Then, touch your feet. Can you do that?

questions.question: How many times should you clap your hands?
What should you touch after clapping?

steps.prompt: Action: Can you say 'Touch your arm'?

questions.question_text: Finish the sentence: Touch your ___. (Grammar question)
Which word is a body part? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: We wave with our leg.
How many arms do people have? (Reading comprehension question)

lessons.title: Ten Fingers
lessons.topic: Small body parts and plural review

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to clap your hands together?
questions.hint: You have two of them, one on each side.

questions.question_text: What do you use to smell a flower?
questions.hint: This part is in the middle of your face.

questions.question_text: What do you use to listen to music?
questions.hint: They are on either side of your head.

items.example: Touch your finger to your nose.
I have a long jump rope.

items.definition: Not big from end to end

steps.title: Wiggle and Clap!
steps.text: Wiggle your fingers! I have ten fingers. Clap your hands! I have ten toes. My toes are short. Touch your toes and smile. This is a happy day for our class.

questions.question: How many fingers do they have?
What should you do after touching your toes?

steps.explanation: We say "are" for more than one.

steps.audio_text: Can you touch your long fingers and count them? Now, let's clap our hands and make some noise!

questions.question: What should you do with your hands?

questions.question_text: Where do you find your toes? (Vocabulary question)
Fingers are ____. (Grammar question)
True or False: 'Toes are short.'
Which sentence is correct? (Reading comprehension)
What do you use to hold a pencil? (Vocabulary question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which body part do you use to jump up?
questions.hint: You use them to run too!

questions.question_text: What do you need to nod your head?
What helps you hold things tightly?

steps.title: Dance with Me!
steps.text: Clap your hands and say, 'I have two hands!' Stomp your feet and shout, 'I have two feet!' Touch your knees and shoulders. Great job, everyone!

questions.question: What do you do with your feet?
questions.locate_text: ' Stomp your feet and shout, 'I have two feet!

questions.question: How many hands do you have?
questions.locate_text: Clap your hands and say, 'I have two hands!

steps.audio_text: Hello friends! Clap your hands and say, 'I have elbows!' Then, tap your knees softly twice!

questions.question: What do you clap and say?
questions.answer: Clap your hands and say 'I have elbows!'
options: Clap your hands and say 'I have elbows!'
Clap your elbows and say 'I have hands!'
Clap your knees and say 'I have feet!'

questions.question: How many times do you tap your knees?

questions.question_text: Which body part do you stomp with? (Vocabulary question)
Clap your ___. (Grammar question)
What body part is mentioned in 'Touch your shoulder'? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: You stomp with your hands.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to see the colors of a rainbow?
Which part of your body helps you smile?
questions.hint: This is where you eat too.

questions.question_text: What do you use to kick a ball?
questions.hint: These are at the end of your legs.

steps.title: Super Day with My Body
steps.text: I have two eyes and a nose. I can see the sky. Touch your nose! I have ten toes. I can stomp my feet. My hands can clap. Touch your shoulders!

questions.question: How many toes does the character have?
What can the character do with hands?

steps.audio_text: Hello! I have a little green frog. It likes to jump and play in the water. Listen to it ribbit and splash!

questions.question: What does the frog like to do?

steps.prompt: Say: 'I have two feet and two legs.'
steps.expected_text: i have two feet and two legs

questions.question_text: Touch your ___ (Vocabulary question)
I have two ___ (Grammar question)
Clap your ___ when you hear the music. (Grammar question)
True or False: 'Boys' is more than one boy. (Grammar question)
What does the girl do when she's happy? (Reading comprehension question)
